name: azure-ai-voicelive-java
description: Real-time bidirectional voice conversations with AI assistants using WebSocket technology. Enables developers to build voice-enabled applications with Azure AI services.
capabilities: Create VoiceLiveAsyncClient for Azure AI VoiceLive service connection
Start and manage WebSocket voice sessions with AI assistants
Configure session options including turn detection and voice selection
Send and receive audio data using reactive streaming patterns
Handle voice events for speech detection and audio playback
Implement function calling within voice conversations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only documentation skill containing code examples for Azure AI VoiceLive SDK. Static analysis found 0 files scanned with 0 potential security issues. Risk score: 0/100. No suspicious patterns detected. Skill provides legitimate documentation for Microsoft Azure service with no executable code.
valueStatement: Developers need a way to integrate real-time bidirectional voice conversations into Java applications. This skill provides complete code examples and best practices for implementing voice AI using Azure AI VoiceLive SDK with WebSocket streaming.
userTitle: Build Real-Time Voice Apps with Azure AI
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and API credentials
Only supports 24kHz 16-bit PCM mono audio format
Depends on Azure network availability for real-time streaming
Requires understanding of reactive Java patterns (Project Reactor)

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise developers building customer service applications
useCases.title: Customer Service Voice Bot
useCases.description: Build an interactive voice assistant for customer support that handles inquiries in real-time using natural speech

useCases.title: Accessibility Tool Development
useCases.description: Create voice-enabled applications for users who prefer voice interaction over text-based interfaces

useCases.targetUser: IoT developers and embedded systems engineers
useCases.title: IoT Voice Control Interface
useCases.description: Implement voice control for IoT devices with low-latency bidirectional communication

promptTemplates.title: Basic Voice Client Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up a basic VoiceLiveAsyncClient in Java with API key authentication using the Azure AI VoiceLive SDK.

promptTemplates.title: Configure Voice Session
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure VoiceLiveSessionOptions with turn detection, voice selection, and audio format settings for a natural conversation flow?

promptTemplates.title: Handle Voice Events
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Java code to handle voice events including speech start/stop detection, audio delta streaming, and error handling in the VoiceLive session.

promptTemplates.title: Implement Function Calling
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to integrate function calling with VoiceLive to enable the AI assistant to execute real actions like weather lookups during conversation.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential instead of API keys for production deployments to leverage Azure managed identities
Configure ServerVadTurnDetection with appropriate threshold and silence duration to match your use case for natural conversation flow
Always implement proper error handling and reconnection logic for production voice applications
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode API keys in source code - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault instead
Avoid blocking calls in reactive streams - use non-blocking patterns throughout
Do not skip audio format validation - ensure input matches 24kHz 16-bit PCM requirements

faq.question: What audio format does Azure AI VoiceLive require?
faq.answer: Azure AI VoiceLive requires 24kHz sample rate, 16-bit PCM, mono channel, signed PCM little-endian format.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure AI VoiceLive?
faq.answer: You can use AzureKeyCredential with API key, or DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity support in production.

faq.question: What voices are available for Azure AI VoiceLive?
faq.answer: The SDK supports OpenAI voices (ALLOY, ASH, BALLAD, CORAL, ECHO, SAGE, SHIMMER, VERSE) and Azure voices including Standard, Custom, and Personal voices.

faq.question: Can I use function calling with VoiceLive?
faq.answer: Yes, you can define functions using VoiceLiveFunctionDefinition and pass them via setTools() in VoiceLiveSessionOptions.

faq.question: How does turn detection work in VoiceLive?
faq.answer: ServerVadTurnDetection uses voice activity detection to automatically detect when the user starts and stops speaking, with configurable threshold and silence duration.

faq.question: What is the difference between TEXT and AUDIO modalities?
faq.answer: TEXT modality sends/receives text, AUDIO modality sends/receives audio. You can combine both using Arrays.asList(InteractionModality.TEXT, InteractionModality.AUDIO).
